* cl-llama-cpp

Common Lisp bindings to [[https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp][llama.cpp]] via CFFI.

** Status

*Work in progress.* The bindings cover the full llama.h C API (auto-generated via CLAW). A higher-level Lisp interface is provided for common inference workflows, while some advanced llama.cpp features currently require using the raw %llama bindings directly. These are the current high-level wrappers:

- Model loading and context creation
- Tokenization / detokenization
- Text generation with sampling (temperature, top-k, top-p, min-p, greedy, and extended samplers)
  - Extended samplers: typical-p, XTC, top-n-sigma, mirostat v1/v2, repetition/frequency/presence penalties, DRY anti-repetition, logit bias, dynamic temperature, adaptive-p
- Token streaming via callback
- Chat template formatting and safe multi-turn tokenization
- Embeddings
- LoRA adapters
- KV cache management (clear, copy, shift, divide, query)
- Model & context introspection (description, metadata, properties, system info)
- Grammar / constrained generation (GBNF grammars, lazy grammars)
- Infill generation

*Not yet wrapped* at the high level (you can use the raw =%llama= bindings directly):

- Batched / parallel decoding
- KV cache state save/restore
- Model quantization
- Speculative decoding
- Multimodal inputs (vision, audio)
- Fine-grained sequence control

The API is unstable and will change without notice.

** Requirements

- A Common Lisp implementation (*SBCL recommended* --- llama.cpp triggers floating-point exceptions that SBCL masks automatically via =sb-int:with-float-traps-masked=; other implementations may work if they do not trap FP exceptions by default, but are untested)
- [[https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp][llama.cpp]] built as a shared library (=libllama.so= / =libllama.dylib=)
- [[https://cffi.common-lisp.dev/][CFFI]] and [[https://github.com/cffi/cffi][cffi-libffi]]

*Platform notes:* Tested on Linux. macOS should work (the library loader handles =.dylib=). Windows is untested and would require building llama.cpp as a DLL.

** Installation

*** Building llama.cpp

The repository includes llama.cpp as a git submodule.

#+begin_src bash
git clone --recursive https://github.com/licjon/cl-llama-cpp
cd cl-llama-cpp/llama.cpp
cmake -B build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
#+end_src

The library loader will automatically find =libllama.so= in =llama.cpp/build/bin/=. Alternatively, install it somewhere on your system library path.

*** Loading with ASDF

Place or symlink this directory into your ASDF source registry (e.g. =~/common-lisp/=), then:

#+begin_src lisp
(asdf:load-system "cl-llama-cpp")
#+end_src

For Roswell users, clone into =~/.roswell/local-projects/= and it will be found automatically.

If you use Quicklisp and want =ql:quickload= to find it, run =ql:register-local-projects= first.

** Quick Start

#+begin_src lisp
(asdf:load-system "cl-llama-cpp")

;; One-shot generation
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 99)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 2048)
    (princ (cl-llama-cpp:generate ctx "The capital of France is"
                                  :max-tokens 64
                                  :temp 0.7))))
#+end_src

*** Multi-turn Chat

The =examples/chat.lisp= file provides a simple interactive chat session:

#+begin_src lisp
(asdf:load-system "cl-llama-cpp/examples")

(cl-llama-cpp/examples/chat:start-chat
  :model-path "/path/to/chat-model.gguf")

(cl-llama-cpp/examples/chat:say "What is Common Lisp?")
;; streams response to *standard-output*

(cl-llama-cpp/examples/chat:say "Tell me more about CLOS.")
;; continues the conversation with history

(cl-llama-cpp/examples/chat:end-chat)
#+end_src

** API Reference

*** Resource Management

- =with-model (var path &rest params) &body body= --- Load a GGUF model, bind to =var=, free on exit. Keyword params override =llama_model_default_params= (e.g. =:n-gpu-layers 99=). Signals =model-load-error= on failure.

- =with-context (var model &rest params) &body body= --- Create an inference context from =model=, bind to =var=, free on exit. Keyword params override =llama_context_default_params= (e.g. =:n-ctx 2048=). Signals =context-creation-error= on failure.

- =with-sampler-chain (var &key temp top-k top-p min-p seed greedy model grammar grammar-lazy grammar-trigger-words infill typical-p xtc-probability xtc-threshold top-n-sigma mirostat mirostat-v2 mirostat-tau mirostat-eta repeat-penalty frequency-penalty presence-penalty penalty-last-n dry-multiplier dry-base dry-allowed-length dry-penalty-last-n dry-seq-breakers logit-bias dynamic-temp-range dynamic-temp-exponent adaptive-p adaptive-p-decay) &body body= --- Build a sampler chain, bind to =var=, free on exit. Grammar, =:mirostat=, =:dry-multiplier=, and =:logit-bias= keywords require =:model=. =:mirostat= and =:mirostat-v2= are mutually exclusive.

*** Operations

- =tokenize model text &key add-special parse-special= --- Tokenize =text= into a vector of token integers.

- =detokenize model tokens &key remove-special unparse-special= --- Convert a token vector back to a string.

- =generate ctx prompt &key max-tokens temp top-k top-p min-p seed parse-special prompt-tokens token-callback grammar grammar-root typical-p xtc-probability xtc-threshold top-n-sigma mirostat mirostat-v2 mirostat-tau mirostat-eta repeat-penalty frequency-penalty presence-penalty penalty-last-n dry-multiplier dry-base dry-allowed-length dry-penalty-last-n dry-seq-breakers logit-bias dynamic-temp-range dynamic-temp-exponent adaptive-p adaptive-p-decay= --- Generate text continuing =prompt=. Returns two values: the generated string and a stop reason (=:eog=, =:length=, or =:callback=). When =prompt-tokens= is provided, uses the pre-tokenized input directly. When =token-callback= is provided, calls it with each decoded token string as it is produced; return =NIL= from the callback to stop generation early. When =grammar= is provided, constrains output to match the GBNF grammar string (=grammar-root= defaults to ="root"=). See the extended sampler keywords below for advanced sampling control.

- =embed ctx text &key normalize= --- Compute embeddings for =text=. Returns a vector of single-floats. The context must be created with =:embeddings 1=.

*** Chat Templates

- =format-chat model messages &key template add-assistant-prefix= --- Format =messages= (a list of =(:role "user" :content "...")= plists) into a prompt string using the model's chat template. Automatically maps the ="assistant"= role for models that expect ="model"= (e.g. Gemma).

- =tokenize-chat model messages &key template add-assistant-prefix= --- Like =format-chat=, but returns a token vector with template markers parsed as special tokens and message content parsed as regular text. This prevents model-generated text that resembles special tokens from corrupting subsequent turns. Use with =generate='s =:prompt-tokens= parameter.

- =model-chat-template model &optional name= --- Return the chat template string embedded in the model.

- =list-chat-templates= --- Return a list of built-in chat template names.

*** LoRA Adapters

- =with-lora (var model path) &body body= --- Load a LoRA adapter GGUF from =path= for =model=, bind to =var=, free on exit. Signals =lora-load-error= on failure.

- =apply-lora ctx adapter &key scale= --- Set the active LoRA adapter on =ctx=. =scale= (default 1.0) controls the adapter strength. Replaces any previously applied adapter. Signals =lora-apply-error= on failure.

- =lora-metadata adapter= --- Return metadata from =adapter= as an alist of =(key . value)= string pairs.

*** Extended Sampler Keywords

These keywords are accepted by =generate=, =with-sampler-chain=, and =build-sampler-chain=. They compose in a fixed order: logit bias → penalties → DRY → grammar → sampling strategy.

*Token filtering:*

| Keyword           | Default | Description                                                |
|-------------------+---------+------------------------------------------------------------|
| =:typical-p=        |         | Keep tokens whose information content is near the expected |
| =:xtc-probability=  |         | Probability of excluding top choices (XTC)                 |
| =:xtc-threshold=    |     0.5 | Minimum probability for a token to be excludable           |
| =:top-n-sigma=      |         | Keep tokens within N standard deviations of the top logit  |
| =:adaptive-p=       |         | Dynamic top-p target probability (experimental)            |
| =:adaptive-p-decay= |     0.0 | Rate at which adaptive-p converges to target               |

*Repetition control:*

| Keyword             | Default | Description                                               |
|---------------------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------------|
| =:repeat-penalty=     |         | Penalise exact token repeats (1.0 = no penalty)           |
| =:frequency-penalty=  |         | Penalty scaling with token frequency (0.0 = off)          |
| =:presence-penalty=   |         | Flat penalty if token appeared at all (0.0 = off)         |
| =:penalty-last-n=     |      64 | How many recent tokens to consider for penalties          |
| =:dry-multiplier=     |         | DRY sequence-repetition penalty strength (requires model) |
| =:dry-base=           |    1.75 | DRY exponential base                                      |
| =:dry-allowed-length= |       2 | Minimum sequence length before DRY applies                |
| =:dry-penalty-last-n= |      -1 | Token window for DRY (-1 = use context size)              |
| =:dry-seq-breakers=   |         | List of strings that reset DRY matching (e.g. newlines)   |
| =:logit-bias=         |         | Alist of =(token-id . bias)= pairs (requires model)         |

*Sampling strategy:*

| Keyword                | Default | Description                                               |
|------------------------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------------|
| =:mirostat=              |         | Enable Mirostat V1 (requires model; replaces normal path) |
| =:mirostat-v2=           |         | Enable Mirostat V2 (replaces normal path)                 |
| =:mirostat-tau=          |     5.0 | Target surprise level (lower = more focused)              |
| =:mirostat-eta=          |     0.1 | Learning rate for mirostat adaptation                     |
| =:dynamic-temp-range=    |         | +/- range for entropy-adaptive temperature                |
| =:dynamic-temp-exponent= |     1.0 | Exponent shaping the dynamic temperature curve            |

*** Sampler Utilities

- =sampler-seed sampler= --- Return the current RNG seed from =sampler= as an integer. Useful for logging the seed that produced a given output.

*** Grammar / Constrained Generation

- =make-grammar-sampler model grammar &key root= --- Create a grammar sampler from a GBNF =grammar= string and =root= rule (default ="root"=). Returns a sampler pointer; caller must free or add to a chain. Signals =grammar-error= if the grammar is empty or invalid.

- =make-grammar-sampler-lazy model grammar &key root trigger-words= --- Create a lazy grammar sampler that stays inactive until a trigger word appears in the output. =trigger-words= is a list of strings (e.g. ='("{")=).

- =make-infill-sampler model= --- Create an infill sampler for fill-in-the-middle generation.

- =with-grammar-sampler (var model grammar &key root) &body body= --- Create a grammar sampler, bind to =var=, free on exit.

*** KV Cache / Memory Management

- =clear-kv-cache ctx= --- Clear all KV cache state for =ctx=.

- =kv-cache-seq-rm ctx seq-id p0 p1= --- Remove cached tokens in positions =[p0, p1)= for =seq-id=. =p0=-1= means from the start, =p1=-1= means to the end. Returns =T= if cells were removed, =NIL= if no matching data.

- =kv-cache-seq-cp ctx src-seq dst-seq p0 p1= --- Copy cached data from =src-seq= to =dst-seq= for positions =[p0, p1)=.

- =kv-cache-seq-keep ctx seq-id= --- Keep only =seq-id='s cached data, removing all other sequences.

- =kv-cache-seq-add ctx seq-id p0 p1 delta= --- Shift positions in =[p0, p1)= for =seq-id= by =delta=.

- =kv-cache-seq-div ctx seq-id p0 p1 d= --- Divide positions in =[p0, p1)= for =seq-id= by =d=. =d= must be non-zero.

- =kv-cache-pos ctx seq-id= --- Return the minimum and maximum cached positions for =seq-id= as =(values min max)=.

- =kv-cache-can-shift-p ctx= --- Return =T= if =ctx='s memory supports position shifting, =NIL= otherwise.

*** Model & Context Introspection

- =model-description model= --- Return the model's description string (e.g. ="gemma3 1B Q4_K - Medium"=).

- =model-metadata model= --- Return all GGUF metadata as an alist of =(key . value)= string pairs.

- =model-info model= --- Return a plist of model properties: =:n-params=, =:n-layers=, =:n-ctx-train=, =:size-bytes=, =:n-heads=, =:n-heads-kv=, =:n-embd-in=, =:n-embd-out=, =:n-swa=, =:rope-type=, =:rope-freq-scale=, =:n-cls-out=, =:encoder-p=, =:decoder-p=, =:recurrent-p=, =:hybrid-p=, =:diffusion-p=.

- =model-cls-label model index= --- Return the classification label string at =index=, or =NIL= if unavailable.

- =context-info ctx= --- Return a plist of context configuration: =:n-ctx=, =:n-batch=, =:n-ubatch=, =:n-seq-max=, =:n-threads=, =:n-threads-batch=, =:pooling-type=.

- =system-info= --- Return a string describing the llama.cpp build and CPU capabilities.

*** Conditions

All conditions inherit from =llama-error=.

| Condition              | Slot    | When                                 |
|------------------------+---------+--------------------------------------|
| =model-load-error=       | =path=    | Model file cannot be loaded          |
| =context-creation-error= |         | Context allocation fails             |
| =tokenization-error=     | =text=    | Tokenization fails                   |
| =decode-error=           | =code=    | =llama_decode= returns non-zero        |
| =chat-template-error=    |         | Chat template rendering fails        |
| =lora-load-error=        | =path=    | LoRA adapter file cannot be loaded   |
| =lora-apply-error=       | =code=    | =set-adapters-lora= returns non-zero   |
| =grammar-error=          | =grammar= | Empty or invalid GBNF grammar string |

*** Low-level Access

The full =llama.h= API is available in the =%llama= package as CFFI function wrappers. See =src/bindings.lisp= for the complete list. These are raw C bindings --- you will need to manage foreign memory and handle llama.cpp calling conventions yourself.

** Examples

*** Tokenization

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 0)
  (let* ((tokens (cl-llama-cpp:tokenize model "Hello, world!"))
         (text   (cl-llama-cpp:detokenize model tokens)))
    (format t "Tokens: ~A~%Round-trip: ~A~%" tokens text)))
#+end_src

*** Streaming Generation

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 99)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 2048)
    (cl-llama-cpp:generate ctx "Once upon a time"
                           :max-tokens 128
                           :temp 0.9
                           :token-callback (lambda (tok)
                                            (write-string tok)
                                            (force-output)
                                            t))))
#+end_src

*** Embeddings

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/embedding-model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 0)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 512 :embeddings 1 :pooling-type 1)
    (let ((vec (cl-llama-cpp:embed ctx "Hello, world!")))
      (format t "~D dimensions~%" (length vec)))))
#+end_src

*** LoRA Adapters

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 99)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 4096)
    (cl-llama-cpp:with-lora (adapter model "/path/to/adapter.gguf")
      (cl-llama-cpp:apply-lora ctx adapter :scale 1.0)
      (let* ((messages (list (list :role "user" :content "Hello!")))
             (tokens (cl-llama-cpp:tokenize-chat model messages)))
        (cl-llama-cpp:generate ctx nil
          :prompt-tokens tokens
          :max-tokens 256
          :token-callback (lambda (tok)
                            (write-string tok)
                            (force-output)
                            t))))))
#+end_src

See =examples/lora.lisp= for a complete walkthrough including adapter download and GGUF conversion.

*** Grammar-Constrained Generation

#+begin_src lisp
;; Force JSON output with a GBNF grammar
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 99)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 2048)
    (cl-llama-cpp:generate ctx "Output a JSON object describing a cat."
                           :max-tokens 128 :temp 0.3
                           :grammar "root    ::= \"{\" ws members ws \"}\"
members ::= pair (\",\" ws pair)*
pair    ::= string ws \":\" ws value
value   ::= string | number
string  ::= \"\\\"\" [a-zA-Z0-9_ ]* \"\\\"\"
number  ::= [0-9]+
ws      ::= [ ]*")))
;; => "{\"name\": \"Whiskers\", \"age\": 3, \"color\": \"gray\"}"
#+end_src

See =examples/sampler-showcase.lisp= for demos of strict grammars, lazy grammars with trigger words, and error handling.

*** Extended Samplers

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 99)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 2048)
    ;; Mirostat V2 — adaptive perplexity-targeted sampling
    (cl-llama-cpp:generate ctx "Invent a mythological creature:"
                           :max-tokens 128
                           :mirostat-v2 t :mirostat-tau 5.0)

    ;; Repetition penalties + DRY for diverse, non-repetitive text
    (cl-llama-cpp:generate ctx "Describe five flowers:"
                           :max-tokens 256 :temp 0.7
                           :repeat-penalty 1.2
                           :frequency-penalty 0.4
                           :presence-penalty 0.4
                           :dry-multiplier 0.8
                           :dry-seq-breakers '("\n" "." ":"))

    ;; Ban specific tokens via logit bias
    (let* ((bad-tokens (cl-llama-cpp:tokenize model "red" :add-special nil))
           (biases (map 'list (lambda (tok) (cons tok -100.0)) bad-tokens)))
      (cl-llama-cpp:generate ctx "Name three colors:"
                             :max-tokens 32 :temp 0.3
                             :logit-bias biases))

    ;; Inspect sampler seed for reproducibility
    (cl-llama-cpp:with-sampler-chain (chain :temp 0.8 :seed 42)
      (format t "Seed: ~A~%" (cl-llama-cpp:sampler-seed chain)))))
#+end_src

See =examples/sampler-comparison.lisp= for a side-by-side comparison of all extended sampler strategies.

*** KV Cache Management

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 99)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 4096)
    ;; After decoding a system prompt on seq 0, share it with seq 1
    (cl-llama-cpp:kv-cache-seq-cp ctx 0 1 0 -1)

    ;; Query cached positions for a sequence
    (multiple-value-bind (min max)
        (cl-llama-cpp:kv-cache-pos ctx 1)
      (format t "Seq 1 cached: [~D .. ~D]~%" min max))

    ;; Sliding window: remove old tokens, shift remaining back
    (cl-llama-cpp:kv-cache-seq-rm ctx 0 0 128)
    (cl-llama-cpp:kv-cache-seq-add ctx 0 128 -1 -128)

    ;; Keep only seq 0, discard all others
    (cl-llama-cpp:kv-cache-seq-keep ctx 0)

    ;; Clear everything
    (cl-llama-cpp:clear-kv-cache ctx)))
#+end_src

See =examples/kv-cache.lisp= for a full multi-user chat server simulation with assert-driven verification.

*** Model Introspection

#+begin_src lisp
(cl-llama-cpp:with-model (model "/path/to/model.gguf" :n-gpu-layers 0)
  (cl-llama-cpp:with-context (ctx model :n-ctx 2048)
    ;; Model description
    (format t "Model: ~A~%" (cl-llama-cpp:model-description model))

    ;; Numeric/boolean properties
    (let ((info (cl-llama-cpp:model-info model)))
      (format t "Parameters: ~D, Layers: ~D, Training ctx: ~D~%"
              (getf info :n-params) (getf info :n-layers) (getf info :n-ctx-train)))

    ;; Context configuration
    (let ((info (cl-llama-cpp:context-info ctx)))
      (format t "Context: ~D tokens, ~D threads~%"
              (getf info :n-ctx) (getf info :n-threads)))

    ;; GGUF metadata
    (dolist (entry (cl-llama-cpp:model-metadata model))
      (format t "  ~A = ~A~%" (car entry) (cdr entry)))))
#+end_src

See =examples/introspection.lisp= for a formatted report of all introspection data.

** Running Tests

Smoke tests run without a model:

#+begin_src bash
sbcl --eval '(ql:quickload "cl-llama-cpp/tests")' \
     --eval '(asdf:test-system "cl-llama-cpp/tests")' \
     --eval '(quit)'
#+end_src

Integration tests require GGUF model files:

#+begin_src bash
LLAMA_TEST_MODEL=/path/to/model.gguf \
LLAMA_TEST_EMBED_MODEL=/path/to/embedding-model.gguf \
LLAMA_TEST_LORA=/path/to/adapter.gguf \
sbcl --eval '(ql:quickload "cl-llama-cpp/tests")' \
     --eval '(asdf:test-system "cl-llama-cpp/tests")' \
     --eval '(quit)'
#+end_src
